a: A personal AI platform with persistent memory, available on WhatsApp, Android, and Web — with a self-hosted option for full control.

q: How does the persistent memory work?
a: Every conversation is embedded and stored in a pgvector database. Your AI retrieves relevant memories through RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) at query time — memories persist until you delete them.

q: Does the AI train on my data?
a: No — your conversations are never used to train the base model. RAG only retrieves your data at query time to answer you.
Yes — the full stack is open-source and ships as a Docker Compose setup (PostgreSQL + pgvector, Redis, a Next.js dashboard, and a LangChain-based agent engine).

q: Does it work with Ollama or other local models?
a: Yes — point it at your local Ollama instance, or connect any OpenAI-compatible model.
Yes. Memory and messages are encrypted at rest and in transit (AES-256), and data is hosted in AWS Mumbai (ap-south-1) unless you choose otherwise.
English, Hindi, Malayalam, and Tamil today, with more Indian languages on the roadmap.

q: Can I use PersonalAI on WhatsApp?
a: Yes — message your AI on WhatsApp directly. Conversations sync with the Web and Android apps.

q: What AI providers can I connect?
a: Bring your own key for OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Mistral, Gemini, DeepSeek, or NVIDIA NIM — or run fully local with Ollama.
Yes — delete individual memories or wipe everything from the dashboard at any time.

q: What's included in the free tier?
a: 1 agent, 500 memory items, and WhatsApp & Web access — no credit card required.

q: Can I use it with Cursor or Claude Code?
a: Yes — point the base URL to your PersonalAI endpoint and it works like any OpenAI-compatible provider.
